name: Rui Zhi (Xuansi)
page_title: Rui Zhi (Xuansi) 芮祉 (宣嗣) [Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
historical_notes:
Rui Zhi, a follower of Sun Jian, was recommended to the position of Administrator of Jiujiang where he presumably held office under the purview of Yuan Shu. He was later transferred to Wu and was regarded well for his administration of both commanderies. It seems he died in the early 190s. (1)

1) de Crespigny, Rafe; A Bibliographical Dictionary of Later Han to the Three Kingdoms (AD 23–220)

full_text:
Rui Zhi (Xuansi) 芮祉 (宣嗣)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Rui Zhi (芮祉), styledXuansi (宣嗣), was born in Danyang. An officer of Sun Jian and Sun Ce. A man from Danyang. Rui Zhi was affiliated with the Wu Kingdom. Rui Zhi lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.

BiographyHistoric (Confirmed)
Rui Zhi, a follower of Sun Jian, was recommended to the position of Administrator of Jiujiang where he presumably held office under the purview of Yuan Shu. He was later transferred to Wu and was regarded well for his administration of both commanderies. It seems he died in the early 190s. (1)
